summary: Split the tail of lbmthp into a new matching lb_01F8 translation unit, moved the THP texture/SObj helper functions and their bss/sdata2 into that split, and fixed several lb data/string/layout details. The bot report showed +1232 matched code bytes, +480 matched data bytes, and 13 new matches, with apparent lbmthp regressions corresponding to functions moved into lb_01F8.

key_files.why: Shortened lbmthp ranges and introduced melee/lb/lb_01F8.c for text 0x8001F890-0x8001FC08, bss 0x804335B8-0x80433658, and sdata2 0x804D7CE0-0x804D7CE8.
Added Object(Matching, "melee/lb/lb_01F8.c") while lbmthp remained NonMatching.
New matching unit containing lbl_804335B8_t and lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, lbMthp8001FAA0.
Removed the moved tail functions and adjusted codegen-sensitive details in fn_8001E910, fn_8001EF5C, and fn_8001F13C.
Changed THPVideoDecode third parameter to void* and promoted lbl_804333E0_t layout with GXTexObj fields and STATIC_ASSERT.
Stopped owning the shared struct definition and the lbl_804335B8 bss object after the split.
Broke aggregate lb/lbshadow/lbsnap/lbmthp data blobs into correctly sized individual string/SJIS labels.
Adjusted __FILE__, Vec3 constness, and local float initializers for lbspdisplay rodata/code matching.
Refined Unk803BACC8 layout and initializer to improve lbsnap data matching.
Matched assertion condition/string data, including the original typo-like text.
Changed HSD_ASSERTREPORT condition from NULL to 0 for matching.

what_changed: Added a new source file src/melee/lb/lb_01F8.c and registered it as a matching object.
Moved lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, and lbMthp8001FAA0 out of lbmthp.c into lb_01F8.c.
Moved the 0xA0-byte lbl_804335B8_t static bss state into lb_01F8.c and kept a STATIC_ASSERT on its size.
Updated GALE01 splits so lbmthp text ends at 0x8001F890 and lb_01F8 owns 0x8001F890-0x8001FC08, plus the corresponding bss and sdata2 tail ranges.
Changed THPVideoDecode declaration from s32 third argument to void* and updated call sites/casts accordingly.
Moved struct lbl_804333E0_t from lbmthp.static.h to lbmthp.h and added the GXStruct include needed for GXTexObj fields.
In lbmthp.c, changed one Movieplayer access pattern from a stack copy to a pointer, removed a newline from the sugano assert report string, and adjusted formatting/casts around HSD_ASSERTREPORT.

In symbols.txt, split lbShadow_803BA0FC from size 0x54 to size 0x30 plus lbl_803BA12C, split lbSnap_803BAD00 into four string/SJIS labels, and split lbmthp string arrays at 0x803BADB0 and 0x803BAE3C into individual lbl_* strings.
In lb_00F9.c, overrode __FILE__ to "lbspdisplay.c", made two Vec3 objects static const, and swapped far/ortho_bot initial values.
In lbsnap.static.h, changed Unk803BACC8 so x1C is void*, added x20 and padding, and inserted an initializer 0 before the -1 pointer.
In lbrefract.c and lbshadow.c, changed HSD_ASSERTREPORT false conditions from NULL to 0; lbshadow also changed the report text to "coudn t get light position ...\n".
smart_moves: Recognized that the nearly matched lbmthp tail actually belonged in a separate address-named translation unit; moving the split converted the three moved functions, .bss, and .sdata2 into full lb_01F8 matches.
Updated splits.txt and configure.py together, avoiding the common mistake of moving source without giving the linker/decomp configuration a matching object and section ownership.
Kept unknown names address-based instead of inventing semantics, while still separating aggregate data into exact labels so the matcher could account for each string and SJIS blob.
Promoted only the needed struct layout to the public header while leaving static object ownership in the static header/new source file.
Preserved codegen-sensitive string details, including missing/newline differences and typo-like assertion text, instead of normalizing them for readability.
Used a pointer to Movieplayer rather than copying the entire struct in fn_8001E910, matching the original access pattern and improving that function without broad rewrites.
decomp_lessons: When a block of functions at the end of a nonmatching file has its own bss/sdata2 tail, try a translation-unit split at the exact text boundary before over-tuning local code.
Move split boundaries in all relevant sections, not just .text; here bss and sdata2 ownership changed with the new lb_01F8 object.
Data matching can require splitting symbol-table aggregate arrays into individual strings with exact sizes and encodings such as data:string versus data:sjis.
Original assert/report strings are data, not comments: exact punctuation, typos, and trailing newlines can determine .data/.sdata matches.
Header promotion is useful when multiple files need a struct type, but keep actual static symbols scoped to their owning translation unit where possible.
Pointer-typed library prototypes matter for matching; changing THPVideoDecode's third argument to void* made call sites represent decode buffers directly.
Small C-source details such as static const, __FILE__ overrides, and local initializer order can move constants between rodata/sdata2 and affect matches.
naming_conventions: New unknown source split was named by address as lb_01F8.c.
Moved functions retained existing lbMthp address-style names: lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, and lbMthp8001FAA0.
Unknown data labels used address-based lbl_* names such as lbl_803BAD00 and lbl_803BAE3C.
Subsystem-owned data labels kept subsystem prefixes where already known, such as lbShadow_803BA0FC and lbSnap_803BACC8.
Struct layouts used address-style temporary names and offset fields, e.g. struct lbl_804335B8_t with x6C, x6E, unk94, and pad_9C.
Known diagnostic strings used str_* naming when available, e.g. str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported.
__FILE__ was explicitly set to the original source filename string "lbspdisplay.c" even though the current file is lb_00F9.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Created a matching object for lb_01F8 while leaving lbmthp as NonMatching, converting moved code into 100% matched items without claiming all of lbmthp.
Used exact split addresses: lbmthp .text 0x8001E910-0x8001F890 and lb_01F8 .text 0x8001F890-0x8001FC08.
Used exact bss split: lbmthp 0x804333E0-0x804335B8 and lb_01F8 0x804335B8-0x80433658.
Used exact sdata2 split: lbmthp 0x804D7CC8-0x804D7CE0 and lb_01F8 0x804D7CE0-0x804D7CE8.
Replaced use of lbl_804D7CE0 in moved code with 0.0F literals in lb_01F8.c to fit the new object's sdata2 ownership.
Changed THPVideoDecode call arguments so decode buffers are passed as void* rather than s32.
Made lb_803B72A8 and lb_803B72B4 static const Vec3 in lb_00F9.c to match read-only data placement.
Changed HSD_ASSERTREPORT conditions from NULL to integer 0 where the generated code/data required it.
Adjusted struct Unk803BACC8 field types and padding so the lbsnap initializer matched the target data layout.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice.

decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code 66.55% (+0.03%, +1232 bytes), linked code 38.85% (+0.02%, +888 bytes), matched data 38.85% (+0.04%, +480 bytes), and linked data 33.15% (+0.01%, +168 bytes).

Bot listed 13 new matches, including lb_01F8 functions lbMthp8001F928, lbMthp8001FAA0, lbMthp8001F890, lb_01F8 .bss/.sdata2, lb_00F9 .rodata, lbshadow data/sdata, lbsnap data, lbrefract sdata, and lbmthp fn_8001F13C.
Bot listed lbmthp regressions for lbMthp8001F928, lbMthp8001FAA0, and lbMthp8001F890, but these correspond to the same functions being removed from lbmthp and rematched under lb_01F8.
follow_up_queries: HSD_ASSERTREPORT NULL 0 coudn t get light position
